diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on radiographic findings showing severe pseudarthrosis of the tibia with bone atrophy, a 12 cm shortening, and femorotibial and femoropatellar dislocation. The patient also had type 1 neurofibromatosis, which is associated with PCT in 40 to 80% of cases. The initial treatment involved orthopedic measures, but the condition progressed to confirmed pseudarthrosis, leading to surgical interventions.
